methods.description: This method involves performing correlation and regression analysis on data that may not be normally distributed. Transformations or rank correlation coefficients are used to improve the analysis results.

steps.description: Apply a logarithmic or square root transformation to the variable to bring the distribution closer to normal.
steps.searchable_summary: apply logarithmic or square root transformation to data

steps.description: Compute rank correlation coefficients that do not make assumptions about the underlying distribution.
Fit non-linear (parabolic, cubic) functions to the tree-ring time series.
steps.searchable_summary: fit non-linear functions to time series

methods.description: This method involves performing spatiotemporal analysis of climate reconstructions using spectral, autocorrelation, and wavelet analysis.

steps.description: Perform simple spectral analysis to examine the strength of a periodic signal in time series at different time frequencies.
steps.searchable_summary: perform spectral analysis on time series

steps.description: Perform autocorrelation analysis to describe cyclicity in time series.
Perform wavelet analysis to analyze the evolution over time of the periodic signal.
steps.searchable_summary: perform wavelet analysis on time series

methods.description: This method involves selecting appropriate station data for dendroclimatic analysis, considering factors like time series length, proximity, and elevation.

steps.description: Select the non-adjusted station with the longest available time series for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select station data with longest time series

steps.description: Offer three plots of the absolute value time series, the annual cycle of the climatic variable, and the time series of anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: generate plots for time series data

steps.description: High-pass filter the selected time series to eliminate low-frequency variability and trends.
